kind: PROJECTION grounded in measured per-node fast-resultant times
machine: single-thread wall-clock on the dev host (Darwin 25.5.0)
field: KoalaBear p = 2^31 - 2^24 + 1 = 2130706433 (31-bit, stored as u32 = 4 bytes/elem)
method_summary: zt_resultant_fast::resultant_fast is a half-GCD driven univariate resultant, validated EXACTLY == pl::resultant (schoolbook oracle) by gates G0..G5 and re-confirmed here on REAL Poseidon zero-test residuals at deg_y up to 3^13. The per-node cost at the RF=6 bounty scale was MEASURED (not extrapolated) on real residual pairs (f0,f1) built via the validated G4 spike route (full_cofactor seed=0, residual_polys_at_x_spike, x=1) through the current-record RP=8 compute-only delta; RP=9 uses the fitted fast-resultant exponent. The zero-test bounty cost is then projected as compute_only = D_I * measured_or_fit_fast_per_node, with D_I = 3^(2*(RF-1)+RP) interpolation nodes and per-node resultant degree delta = 3^(RF-1+RP).
measurement_mode: core_rs/src/bin/zt_resultant_fast.rs subcommand: `time-real <rf> <rp> [--schoolbook]` builds ONE real residual at (rf,rp), times resultant_fast, optionally times+matches schoolbook pl::resultant. Emits JSON {rf,rp,deg_y,fast_s,schoolbook_s,match,build_s}.
degree_note: Empirically deg_y(residual) = 3^(rf+rp) for EVEN rf (rf=2/rp=4 -> 3^6=729; rf=4/rp=5 -> 3^9=19683; rf=6/rp=5 -> 3^11=177147; rf=6/rp=6 -> 3^12=531441; rf=6/rp=7 -> 3^13=1594323). Odd rf truncates one full-round layer (rf=5/rp=6 -> 3^10=59049, not 3^11) because half=r_f/2 integer-divides. The per-node projection delta(RF,RP)=3^(RF-1+RP) therefore equals deg_y of a real even-rf residual: delta(6,6)=3^11=deg_y(6,5); delta(6,7)=3^12=deg_y(6,6); delta(6,4)=3^9=deg_y(4,5). So every per-node time below is a REAL residual measured at exactly the projection degree.

deg_531441.note: RF=6/RP=6 scale: fast == schoolbook EXACTLY, schoolbook ran in 107.7s

deg_1594323.note: schoolbook skipped (O(n^2) too slow); fast value=421540444

fast_exponent.model: log-log least squares t = c * deg^k over the task fit set (bench 1024..262144 + real 19683/531441/1594323)
fast_exponent.interpretation: Sub-quadratic and consistent with quasi-linear half-GCD theory. The global power fit lands at ~1.22 (the bench-only fit was ~1.18); the top real decade behaves even better (local k~0.86 from 531441 to 1594323).

fast_531441_vs_extrapolation.deviation: MEASURED 15.5s is ~30% ABOVE the prior ~12s n*log n extrapolation. Real residuals are slightly heavier per-node than the optimistic nlogn projection; the power-fit (k=1.2154) predicts ~14.0s, closer. The projections below use the MEASURED 15.5s, not the 12s extrapolation, so they are conservative w.r.t. that gap.

paper_repro_core_hours_vs_312.reference: ePrint 2026/150 Table 2: Poseidon2-31k RF=6/RP=4 = 2.6h on 120 threads = 312 core-hours (~2^46.3 ops)
paper_repro_core_hours_vs_312.verdict: METHODOLOGY CONFIRMED: our measured-fast-per-node projection reproduces the paper's 312 core-hours to within 0.5% (313.6 vs 312). This anchors the RF=6 projections below.

projections.note: compute_only = D_I * measured_fast_per_node. Each fast_per_node is a REAL residual measured at exactly the projection delta degree.

rf6_rp9.fast_per_node_source: FIT from fast_exponent.value=1.2154; no real degree-3^14 residual timed

memory_model.formula: build-once bivariate coefficient-poly representation ~ delta^2/2 field elements * 4 bytes/elem
memory_model.note: This is the STREAMING/RAM gate. The build-once bivariate reaches ~4.7 TiB at the current-record RF=6/RP=8 compute-only delta and ~42.6 TiB at the first public-record-improving RF=6/RP=9 delta, before any out-of-core overhead. These memory/I/O costs are NOT captured by compute_only.

verdict_rationale: The live Initiative page checked 2026-06-17 reports RF=6/RP=8 as the current zero-test record, making RP=9 the first public-record-improving target. Even the compute-only fast-resultant projection is already ~493 core-years at RP=8 and ~6.9e3 core-years at RP=9, and it still excludes build/interpolation overhead plus assumes perfect 100%-efficient parallelism. More decisively, the build-once bivariate is a multi-TiB to tens-of-TiB streaming gate per the memory model. Net: out of reach as a naive compute bid; the only plausible fresh surface is a structural build collapse, e.g. a vulnerable admissible custom MDS.
caveats: MEMORY/STREAMING GATE: the build-once bivariate is ~delta^2/2 * 4 bytes = 4.7 TiB at RF=6/RP=8 and 42.6 TiB at RF=6/RP=9. These targets cannot hold the bivariate in a single commodity node and require an out-of-core/streaming design whose I/O cost is NOT in the compute-only number.
COMPETITION/RECORD STATUS: RF=6/RP=8 is the current public zero-test record as of the 2026-06-17 Initiative page check; RP=9 is the first public-record-improving target.

SINGLE-RESIDUAL TIMING: each fast_per_node is ONE real residual (x=1, seed=0) timed once on a single thread. Per-node cost varies node-to-node (remainder-sequence shape, zero-remainder shortcuts); the true mean could differ by some constant factor. The deg-531441 schoolbook match (fast==pl::resultant) confirms correctness but not per-node-time variance.

BUILD + INTERPOLATION OVERHEAD NOT INCLUDED: compute_only counts only the per-node resultant. Building each residual (measured 0.9s@177147, 3.6s@531441, 11.7s@1594323) and the final multipoint interpolation over D_I nodes are excluded. Residual-build alone is already comparable to or larger than the resultant at below-record RP=7; for record-improving RP=9 the build/streaming wall dominates the decision.

PARALLEL-EFFICIENCY ASSUMPTION: core-hours assume embarrassingly-parallel nodes at 100% efficiency; real cluster overhead, scheduling, and the streaming bivariate's shared-state contention reduce effective throughput.

EXTRAPOLATION GAP: measured fast@531441 (15.5s) is ~30% above the prior ~12s nlogn extrapolation; if per-node cost keeps drifting above the optimistic model at larger delta, RF=6/RP=7 gets worse, not better. The projections use the measured (heavier) times, so they are conservative against this specific gap but not against further super-model drift.
PROJECTION, NOT A RUN: this artifact is a PROJECTION grounded in measured per-node fast-resultant times at the exact bounty degrees. It is NOT an executed end-to-end zero-test at RF=6.

files.measurement_binary: core_rs/src/bin/zt_resultant_fast.rs (time-real subcommand; validated resultant math + gates G0..G5 unchanged)
repro_commands: zt_resultant_fast time-real 6 6 --schoolbook   # deg_y=531441 (RF6/RP7 delta); confirms fast==schoolbook
zt_resultant_fast time-real 6 7                # deg_y=1594323 (RF6/RP8 current-record delta; schoolbook skipped)
RF6/RP9 uses the fitted fast exponent for deg_y=3^14; no real degree-3^14 residual timed
